task_completion.description: Open the correct surface pressure dataset in Panoply
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Panoply 'Sources' window should show 'pres.mon.ltm.nc' open. It must NOT be a Sea Level Pressure (SLP) dataset.

task_completion.description: Create a map plot for the month of May
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window is open showing surface pressure, with the time/date dimension set to May (e.g., index 5 or explicitly labeled 'May' in the plot title or settings).

task_completion.description: Zoom or center the map on the Himalayan region
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map plot should be visually focused on the region around 28°N, 87°E (near the border of Nepal/China/India), rather than showing a full global map.

task_completion.description: Export the Panoply map as a PNG to the specified directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal window, or Panoply save dialog showing 'himalayan_surface_pressure_may.png' saved in the '/home/ga/Documents/HeliSAR' directory.

task_completion.description: Complete the certification report with the correct dataset pressure value
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing 'pressure_baseline_report.txt' filled out with a pressure value around 69,600 Pa to 72,500 Pa (or 696 to 725 hPa/mb). It must reflect the dataset's value, not a real-world Everest value.

integrity.description: The agent used the Panoply GUI to generate the map
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Panoply application interface being actively used to plot and view the data, rather than a script bypassing the GUI.

integrity.description: The agent did not use Sea Level Pressure data
integrity.visual_evidence: The plotted variable must be Surface Pressure (often 'pres'). There should be no evidence of 'slp.mon.ltm.nc' or 'Sea Level Pressure' being used for the final map or report.

integrity.description: The agent extracted the pressure value from the dataset rather than hallucinating a real-world physics value
integrity.visual_evidence: The report must contain the dataset's coarse-resolution artifact value (~696-725 hPa) rather than a hallucinated real-world high-altitude pressure (e.g., 450-650 hPa).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct dataset is 'pres.mon.ltm.nc' (Surface Pressure). Sea Level Pressure must NOT be used. The target month is May. The map must be exported to '/home/ga/Documents/HeliSAR/himalayan_surface_pressure_may.png'. The report at '/home/ga/Documents/HeliSAR/pressure_baseline_report.txt' must be completed. Note: Due to the 2.5-degree resolution of the dataset, the actual surface pressure value near 28°N, 87°E in the data is approximately 69,600 Pa (interpolated) or 72,500 Pa (nearest grid point 27.5N, 87.5E). This is higher than real-world Everest pressures (45k-65k Pa). The agent must record this dataset-derived value (or its equivalent in hPa/mb, e.g., 696-725 hPa), proving they read it from the tool rather than guessing.
